text:
Traffic flow prediction is one of the key applications of the Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) and has a wide range of use cases in smart cities. The traffic data exhibits highly non-linear and complex spatio-temporal relationships which needs to be modelled accurately in order to have reliable predictions. Although Spatio-temporal Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are recently considered as one of the state-of-the-art solutions for modelling such traffic data, they have many constraints: 1) they assume a static graph structure over locations that may not hold for dynamic data patterns like traffic which can be highly varying; 2) their limited receptive field makes them incapable of effectively capturing distant spatial connections especially when they are not limited to neighbours alone; 3) the intrinsic lag in data flowing from one location to another is usually ignored as it has no effect when data samples from the two locations are paired together during training.

Motivated by the existing limitations, we propose a new model, named PDFormer, which stands for Propagation Delay-aware dynamic long-range Transformer. PDFormer is shown to significantly outperform state-of-the-art methods on six large-scale real-world traffic datasets. The basic idea of our new model is a spatial self-attention mechanism to capture the changing spatial correlations, with two graph masking matrices designed to model the local and global spatial correlations, respectively. More importantly, the proposed traffic delay-aware feature transformation is adopted to model the propagation delay of traffic information. To validate the proposed model, the experiments results are shown, where the proposed model achieves the best performance and the most efficient running speed among all baseline models. Attention maps are also visualized to validate the effectiveness of our model and provide further insights to traffic dynamics modeling.
